name: Cuatro Lobos, Parque Nacional de Monfragüe – circular desde Casas de Millán
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Casas de Millán are situated within the last foothills of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offering diverse terrain for cycling. The region features Mediterranean forests, the characteristic dehesa landscape with scattered trees, and routes through the Sierra de Santa María. This area provides a variety of natural environments, from extensive trail networks to routes with panoramic views. Best mountain bike trails around Casas De Millán The most popular mountain bike trail is Route Legends BTT-short - circular from Casas de Millán, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular exploration of the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cuatro Lobos, Parque Nacional de Monfragüe – circular desde Casas de Millán, a moderate 32.1 miles (51.6 km) path. This route leads through the Monfragüe National Park, known for its rich biodiversity. Local mountain bikers also love the Puente de Alconétar – Tajo Reservoir loop from Cañaveral, a 15.6 miles (25.2 km) trail leading through the Tajo Reservoir area, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Casas De Millán is defined by Mediterranean forests, the dehesa landscape, and routes within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Casas De Millán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Casas De Millán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Casas de Millán?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Casas de Millán, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The network includes routes through Mediterranean forests, the dehesa landscape, and within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What types of landscapes will I encounter while mountain biking in this region?
A: You'll experience a diverse range of landscapes, including Mediterranean forests, the unique dehesa landscape with scattered trees, and routes offering panoramic views in the Sierra de Santa María. The area is also part of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, known for its rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, there are easy mountain bike trails available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Alconétar – Tajo Reservoir loop from Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25.2 km route that takes approximately 2 hours to complete, leading through the Tajo Reservoir area.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes near Casas de Millán?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradilla – El Alambique Viewpoint loop from Barco de Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers nearly 60 km with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Many routes in the region are circular. A notable moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010123700/cuatro-lobos-parque-nacional-de-monfraguee-circular-desde-casas-de-millan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Lobos, Parque Nacional de Monfragüe – circular desde Casas de Millán&lt;/a&gt;, which is 51.6 km long and takes you through the Monfragüe National Park.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region offers several natural attractions. You can find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3789378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alcántara Reservoir and Almonte Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5577981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Alcántara Reservoir and Bridges over the Almonte River&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5876460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Fraile&lt;/a&gt; waterfall and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2704612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;‘Padre Santo’ Cork Oak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking around Casas de Millán?
A: The region is generally suitable for outdoor activities for much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, allowing you to enjoy the trails and natural beauty without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Casas de Millán?
A: Casas de Millán serves as a common starting point for many routes, and you can typically find parking within the village or at designated trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Casas de Millán family-friendly?
A: While some routes are more challenging, there are easier trails that can be suitable for families, especially those with some biking experience. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' to find the most family-friendly options.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Casas de Millán?
A: The mountain bike trails around Casas de Millán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Over 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the opportunity to ride through areas like the Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Can I access the trails using public transport?
A: While Casas de Millán is a starting point for many routes, public transport options directly to specific trailheads might be limited. It's generally recommended to arrive by car for maximum flexibility, especially when planning to explore routes further afield in the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve or Sierra de Santa María.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Lobo&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas, and routes near the Alcántara Reservoir offer impressive views of the water and surrounding landscape.
tips: The origin of the castle of Mirabel or Peña del Acero dates back to the 15th century. Now it is for the most part in ruins. The views of the surroundings from this point are spectacular.
You can sleep in Serradilla in a rural house.
